prompt:
Chapter 1

1:1 Thou hast bid that a writing should go forth, a composition touching upon that burning orb on high, that glorious sun which doth illumin all days of our living.

1:2 And lo, a command was laid upon thy scrivant, saying, Thou shalt not inscrib nor fashion any word containing that fifth symbol of thy script, which standith among thy alphabit; nay, not as a solitary glyph, nor as a part of any word whatsovoir.

1:3 And it was strictly chargd that thou must avoid, with all thy cunning and with all thy skill, any and all markings which harbour that
response:
I notice you're writing in a biblical/archaic style about the sun, with the constraint of avoiding the letter "e" (a lipogram). Let me continue in that spirit:

1:3 And it was strictly chargd that thou must avoid, with all thy cunning and with all thy skill, any and all markings which harbour that common glyph, that fifth symbol of our writing, though it doth lurk in many a word and many a turn of thought.

1:4 For lo, that sun on high, that burning star of gold, doth pour its radiant light upon all lands, upon all mountains and all flowing springs, from dawn's first blush unto dusk's final glow.

1:5 And who among us can gaze upon that orb without shutting fast his sight? For its glory is too vast, too bright, too full of burning for any mortal pupil to withstand.

1:6 It doth grant warmth unto crops of barley and of corn, and doth bid all growing things to push upward from dark soil into its shining domain.

1:7 Without its light, no fruit shall ripen, no lamb shall frolic upon grassy hills, no bird shall sing its morning hymn among blossoming boughs.

1:8 And so this composition shall honour that magnificent orb, that king of sky, that lamp which God Almighty did hang upon high on that fourth day of His grand crafting of all things.

1:9 Sing prais unto it, all nations! Lift thy hands, all kingdoms! For without its burning gift, all living things would fall into cold and dark, into shadow without finish.

1:10 Thus saith thy scrivant: I shall go forth in this task, writing with joy, though that common glyph must stay banishd from my quill forvr and always. Amin.
